title: Jesse Eisenberg on Turning Down Mark Zuckerberg Role in ‘The Social Reckoning’: ‘I’m Moving in Different Directions in My Life’
content:
Jesse Eisenbergopened up toVarietyabout what it was like turning down the opportunity to return as Facebook mastermindMark Zuckerbergin Aaron Sorkin’s “The Social Network” sequel, “The Social Reckoning.”

“It’s an honor to speak to Aaron in any capacity, because he’s so articulate and charming and so bright,” Eisenberg toldVarietyat Sunday’s Los Angeles premiere of “Minions & Monsters.” “We talked about doing the movie for several days. The way Aaron speaks, he speaks so wonderfully, as he writes that, in a way, if you’re not going to do something with him, it feels almost like you’re letting down America.”

He added, “I just told him I’m moving in different directions in my life, and you know, what he said sums it up nicely. I don’t want to be associated with that character, but all of my reasons for not wanting to do the movie have nothing to do with how wonderful the movie is, and will be, and I’m sure is already.”

Jesse Eisenberg recalls telling Aaron Sorkin he didn’t want to reprise his role as Mark Zuckerberg in “The Social Reckoning,” saying he was “moving in different directions” and no longer wanted to be “associated with the character”:“The way Aaron speaks, in a way, if you’re not…pic.twitter.com/qYkQTXgcsO

Earlier in June, Sorkin told Vanity Fair that hespent three days trying to convince Eisenbergto return as Zuckerberg, but ultimately, he no longer wanted to be “conflated” with the tech mogul.

“I felt like it belonged to him, and he was certainly battle-tested,” Sorkin said. “He simply did not want to be conflated with Mark Zuckerberg anymore, that he has his problems with the guy. He doesn’t like kids coming up to him in airports with business cards that say ‘I’m CEO, bitch’ for him to sign.”

“The Social Reckoning,” which sees Jeremy Strong step into the role of Zuckerberg, follows Facebook engineer Frances Haugen (Mikey Madison) and Wall Street Journal reporter Jeff Horwitz (Jeremy Allen White), whose reporting scrutinized the company’s internal workings. A 2021 investigative series exposed Facebook’s damaging effects on teens and its role in circulating misinformation, including content linked to political violence.
title_zh: 傑西·艾森伯格(Jesse Eisenberg)談到拒絕在《社交危機》(The Social Reckoning)中飾演馬克·祖克柏(Mark Zuckerberg)的角色：“我在生活中正朝著不同的方向前進。”
content_zh:
傑西·艾森伯格(Jesse Eisenberg)向《綜藝》(Variety)透露，拒絕回歸擔任Facebook大亨馬克·祖克柏(Mark Zuckerberg)的機會，參演亞倫·索金(Aaron Sorkin)的《社群網戰》(The Social Network)續集《社群覺醒》(The Social Reckoning)的感受。

“能以任何身份與亞倫交談是一種榮幸，因為他非常口才出眾、迷人且聰明，”艾森伯格在洛杉磯舉行的《小小兵與怪獸》(Minions & Monsters)首映式上告訴《綜藝》。 “我們談了幾天有關拍攝這部電影的事。亞倫的表達方式非常美妙，正如他的寫作一樣，若不與他合作，會讓人感覺幾乎像是辜負了美國。”

他補充道：“我只是告訴他我在生活中朝不同的方向發展，你知道，他所說的話總結得很好。我不想與那個角色有任何關聯，但我不想拍這部電影的所有理由都與這部電影的精彩程度無關，我相信它已經很精彩了，將來也會更精彩。”

傑西·艾森伯格回憶起告訴亞倫·索金他不想在《社群覺醒》中重返馬克·祖克柏的角色，表示他正在“朝不同的方向發展”，不再想與這個角色“有關聯”：“亞倫的表達方式，若你不…”

在六月份早些時候，索金告訴《名利場》(Vanity Fair)，他花了三天時間試圖說服艾森伯格回歸擔任祖克柏，但最終他不再想與這位科技大亨“混為一談”。

“我覺得這個角色屬於他，而他無疑經歷過考驗，”索金說。“他只是再也不想與馬克·祖克柏混為一談，因為他對這個人有自己的問題。他不喜歡孩子們在機場找他簽名，名片上寫著‘我是CEO，混蛋’。”

《社群覺醒》由傑瑞米·強(Jeremy Strong)擔任祖克柏一角，故事圍繞Facebook工程師弗朗西斯·豪根(Frances Haugen，米基·麥迪遜飾)和《華爾街日報》記者傑夫·霍維茲(Jeff Horwitz，傑瑞米·艾倫·懷特飾)，他們的報導深入檢視了公司的內部運作。2021年的一系列調查揭露了Facebook對青少年的有害影響及其在散播錯誤資訊中的角色，包括與政治暴力相關的內容。
summary_zh: 傑西·艾森伯格(Jesse Eisenberg)向《綜藝》(Variety)透露，拒絕回歸擔任Facebook大亨馬克·祖克柏(Mark Zuckerberg)的機會，參演亞倫·索金(Aaron Sorkin)的《社群網戰》(The Social Network)續集《社群覺醒》(The Social Reckoning)的感受。
